finding: A queue requirement and its later completion are distinct temporal claims. The later state must supersede the current-status interpretation of the earlier record without erasing the earlier observation.

source_status.earlier: CM-0003 remains an observed historical queue requirement tied to its original ACTIVE_QUEUE blob.
source_status.later: CM-0009 is an observed completion-state record corroborated by the current ACTIVE_QUEUE blob and implementation commits.

observed: M-001 is completed in the current queue
The earlier required-state record remains present
CM-0009 links to CM-0003 through supersedes
inferred: Explicit temporal supersession reduces the risk of treating stale queue state as current
proposed: Apply the same append-only temporal rule to future deployment, naming, and capability-status changes
superseded: Reading CM-0003 as the current queue state
unresolved: No authentic contradiction-linked pair has yet been added

privacy_boundary: Public repository files and commit metadata only; no private chat, health, credential, contact, or unreviewed upload content.
verification_route: Confirm CM-0003 remains byte-preserved as a historical record
falsification_route: Reject the supersession if the referenced current ACTIVE_QUEUE blob does not mark M-001 completed, if CM-0003 is removed or altered, or if the graph validator reports a dangling, self, or cyclic relation.
